title: melissa rugosa
intro: Melissa rugosa, also known as the small-leaved lemon balm or rugosa lemon balm, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to Europe and Asia. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal medicine for their calming and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia. The medicinal properties of Melissa rugosa include its ability to act as a natural antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, making it beneficial for treating digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory issues. Melissa rugosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or used topically in creams and ointments to reap its health benefits.
